system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Front face (z = 0)
    (-1 -1 0)   // 0
    ( 3 -1 0)   // 1
    ( 3  1 0)   // 2
    (-1  1 0)   // 3

    // Back face (z = 0.1)
    (-1 -1 0.1) // 4
    ( 3 -1 0.1) // 5
    ( 3  1 0.1) // 6
    (-1  1 0.1) // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (180 60 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              0.01;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalisedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;
        tauStar 1;
    }
}')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword viscosityModel is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_Bmodel/runs/Cylinder/1/constant/momentumTransport/laminar"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_Bmodel/runs/Cylinder/1/constant/momentumTransport/laminar from line 13 to line 22.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword viscosityModel is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/momentumTransport/laminar"
```

This means that when the `CrossPowerLaw` viscosity model is selected, the solver expects a `viscosityModel` keyword to be present directly inside the `laminar` dictionary (or within the `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary). However, looking at the current `momentumTransport` file, the `CrossPowerLaw` model is specified using a `viscosityModel` sub-dictionary nested inside `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs`.

In OpenFOAM's `crossPowerLaw` viscosity model, the solver actually looks for the `viscosityModel` keyword **and its associated parameters** (`nuInf`, `m`, `n`, etc.) directly within the main `laminar` dictionary, not nested inside a `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary.

Looking at the reference tutorial `offsetCylinder`, the `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary contains additional metadata like `tauStar`, but the viscosity model parameters themselves (`viscosityModel`, `nuInf`, `m`, `n`) should be at the same level as `laminarModel` in the `laminar` dictionary.

## Proposed Fix

### Modify `constant/momentumTransport` — Move viscosity model parameters to the top level

The `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary should contain only the `tauStar` parameter (which controls the transition/shear-rate threshold), while the viscosity model settings (`viscosityModel`, `nuInf`, `m`, `n`) should be placed directly inside the `laminar` dictionary alongside `laminarModel`.

The corrected structure should be:

```
laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalisedNewtonian;

    viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

    nuInf   10;
    m       0.4;
    n       3;

    tauStar 1;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        tauStar 1;
    }
}
```

Key changes:
1. Add `viscosityModel CrossPowerLaw;` directly inside the `laminar` dictionary
2. Add the parameters `nuInf 10;`, `m 0.4;`, `n 3;` directly inside the `laminar` dictionary
3. Keep `tauStar 1;` at the top level (the solver reads it here)
4. The `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary can remain with `tauStar 1;` inside it (this is the standard tutorial format)

Alternatively, if the above approach doesn't work as expected, another valid approach is to place all parameters (`viscosityModel`, `nuInf`, `m`, `n`, `tauStar`) directly inside `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` without nesting, and remove the separate top-level `viscosityModel`, `nuInf`, `m`, `n`, `tauStar` entries. But the most likely fix is moving the viscosity model parameters to the top level as shown above.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/momentumTransport', 'changes': 'Move viscosityModel CrossPowerLaw and parameters nuInf 10, m 0.4, n 3 to the top level of the laminar dictionary, keeping laminarModel generalisedNewtonian and tauStar 1; Move nuInf, m, n entries to be direct children of laminar alongside laminarModel; Remove the generalizedNewtonianCoeffs sub-dictionary entirely since all its content was moved to the top level'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 0.01 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
